hooks.text: You think you paid your supplier in full. The three letters your bank picked for you mean they got less, and you never agreed to it.
There are three letters hidden on every wire you send to China. Your bank chooses them for you, and one of them quietly costs your supplier money.
Every bank between you and your supplier can take up to fifty dollars off the same wire. With the default code, that comes straight out of what your supplier was supposed to get.
A business owner wires the exact invoice amount to China. The supplier emails back saying it is short. Nobody stole it. A three-letter code on the wire decided who pays the banks in between.
If you have ever sent money to China, your supplier may have been shorted on every single wire. One default code you never see is taking it, payment after payment.
